title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 - 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The cave paintings found at Lascaux in France and Altamira in Spain are among the earliest surviving examples of human artistic expression, (0) which have been dated to between fifteen and forty thousand years ago. Their discovery transformed scholarly understanding of the cognitive capacities of early humans. (9) Although interpreting the meaning or purpose of Palaeolithic art remains deeply uncertain, there is broad agreement that its creation required sophisticated planning and symbolic thinking. (10) Such was the technical skill displayed by Palaeolithic artists that some researchers argued for a sudden cognitive revolution in human prehistory, though this view has since been questioned. The artists worked in challenging conditions deep inside cave systems, and (11) Provided that they had access to pigments made from iron oxides and charcoal, they could produce images of remarkable precision and movement. Not (12) only do these paintings depict animals with anatomical accuracy, but some also suggest an understanding of perspective and the representation of motion. (13) Regardless of the precise social or ritual context in which they were created, these images survive as evidence of minds already fully modern in their imaginative range. Not (14) until the development of portable art forms did human visual culture break free of its dependence on fixed cave surfaces. (15) Without the accidental discoveries made by amateur explorers in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries, these extraordinary images might have remained unknown to science for far longer. Their continued study reveals new layers of meaning with each generation of researchers. (16) Whether they were made for ritual, narrative or purely aesthetic purposes, these paintings remain a profound testimony to the depth of human imagination.

example.text: The cave paintings found at Lascaux in France and Altamira in Spain are among the earliest surviving examples of human artistic expression, (0) which have been dated to between fifteen and forty thousand years ago.

questions.explanation: Concessive conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession between two clauses.
Emphatic determiner in the inverted structure 'Such was/is the [noun] that...' to express a high degree.
Conditional conjunction meaning 'on the condition that', used to introduce a condition and its result.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also', which requires subject-auxiliary inversion after 'Not only'.
Part of the fixed prepositional phrase 'Regardless of', meaning 'without being affected by'.
Part of the 'Not until' negative fronting structure, which requires subject-auxiliary inversion.
Preposition used in a conditional-concessive sense, meaning 'if it had not been for' or 'in the absence of'.
Conjunction introducing two alternatives or possibilities in a summarising conclusion.
